qualification.details: All posts are to be filled on deputation/absorption basis only. Only officers/officials already serving in Central Government, State Governments, Union Territories, Courts, Tribunals or Statutory Organisations are eligible. Departmental officers in the feeder grade who are in the direct line of promotion are NOT eligible for deputation. Age must not exceed 56 years as on the closing date of receipt of applications.

salary_details.pay_scale: Level-2 to Level-13 (7th Central Pay Commission Pay Matrix)

selection_process: Basis of Selection Scrutiny of bio-data, service record, APAR/ACR ratings, qualifications, experience and suitability for the post
Channel Application must come through proper channel - Employer/Head of Office/Forwarding Authority must certify all particulars and enclose ACRs/APARs
Tie-Breaking Selection Committee's assessment of overall suitability, ACR/APAR grades and domain experience
Period of Deputation Initially 3 years or till further orders, whichever is earlier
Absorption into NCLT Can be considered after 1 year of deputation with satisfactory performance. NOT a matter of right - based solely on NCLT's need and Recruitment Rules 2020.
GPRA Only officers already availing GPRA in parent body can continue the benefit. Others are not eligible for GPRA on joining NCLT.
Withdrawal of Application NOT permitted after submission. Candidates unwilling to join upon selection should NOT apply.
Governing Rules DoPT O.M. No. 6/8/2009-Estt.(Pay II) dated 17.06.2010 as amended
Selection will be based on scrutiny of applications and supporting documents submitted through proper channel
followed by assessment by the Selection Committee. There is no written examination.
how_to_apply: Candidates shall apply offline only using Prescribed Application Proforma (Annexure-II). Applications must be submitted through proper channel only - via the Employer/Head of Office/Forwarding Authority. Applications sent directly by the candidate without being forwarded through proper channel will not be considered. Incomplete applications or those not accompanied by required documents will not be considered.
Step 1: Download Application Proforma (Annexure-II) and eligibility conditions (Annexure-I) from www.nclt.gov.in under "Career".
Step 2: Fill in Annexure-II completely. Attach self-attested passport size coloured photograph. State clearly in Section 19 how you are best suited for the post.
Step 3: Attach all required documents (see list below).
Step 4: Submit the complete application to your Employer/Head of Office/Forwarding Authority for certification and forwarding.
Step 5: Forwarding authority sends the complete application to the Secretary, NCLT on or before the last date.
Documents Required (to be submitted through proper channel):
Bio-data in Prescribed Proforma - Annexure-II (completely filled)
Supporting certificates/documents for claimed educational qualifications including essential Bachelor's Degree
Cadre Clearance Certificate - certifying the officer will be relieved immediately upon selection
Attested photocopies of APARs/ACRs for the last 5 years - attested by Under Secretary rank or above
Vigilance Clearance Certificate issued by the respective department
Integrity Certificate issued by the respective department
Details of major/minor penalties imposed during the last 10 years

Employer/Head of Office forwarding certificate (as per Annexure-II format) confirming: no vigilance/disciplinary/criminal case pending; integrity certified; APAR enclosed; no penalty in last 10 years; cadre authority has no objection
Send Complete Application By Post To: The Secretary, NCLT National Company Law Tribunal 6th Floor, Block No. 3, C.G.O. Complex Lodhi Road, New Delhi – 110003
important_instructions: Applications must be submitted through proper channel only - via Employer/Head of Office/Forwarding Authority. Direct applications will not be accepted. Candidates who apply will NOT be allowed to withdraw their application subsequently. Candidates unwilling to join upon selection must NOT apply. Absorption into NCLT is NOT a matter of right. It will be done only based on NCLT's need and as per NCLT Recruitment Rules 2020. Absorption can be considered only after 1 year of satisfactory deputation. Vacancy position is tentative and may increase or decrease. Selected candidates will serve only at the specific NCLT Bench for which they are selected. GPRA benefit: Only officers already availing GPRA in their parent body can continue this benefit after joining NCLT on deputation. Others are not eligible to apply for GPRA on joining NCLT. Cadre authorities must verify that all particulars sent by their officers are correct as per official records before forwarding applications. Pre-01.01.2016 regular service will be treated as service in the corresponding level of the 7th CPC pay matrix for eligibility determination. NCLT Recruitment Rules 2020 are available on www.nclt.gov.in under "Career" - candidates must read these before applying. Applications not accompanied by all required documents, or received after the last date, will not be considered under any circumstances. All corrigenda/updates will be published on www.nclt.gov.in - visit regularly.

content_sections.heading: NCLT Deputation Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF
content_sections.text: National Company Law Tribunal, Block No. 3, 6th Floor, C.G.O. Complex, Lodhi Road, New Delhi-110003 has officially released File No. 10/121/2016-NCLT(Pt.VI) dated 21/05/2026 for filling up various posts on deputation/absorption basis at NCLT Benches across India. The notification covers post-wise eligibility conditions, pay levels, bench-wise vacancies, deputation terms, required documents and application instructions. Candidates are strongly advised to read the full notification, Annexure-I (eligibility criteria) and NCLT Recruitment Rules 2020 available on the official website before applying. Download NCLT Deputation Recruitment 2026 Official Notification

content_sections.heading: Who should NOT apply for NCLT Deputation Recruitment 2026?
content_sections.text: Candidates not currently serving as regular officers in Central/State Government, UTs, Courts, Tribunals or Statutory Organisations - this recruitment is exclusively for serving government employees. Departmental officers in the feeder grade who are in the direct line of promotion for the applied post - explicitly ineligible for deputation. Officers whose age exceeds 56 years as on the closing date of receipt of applications. Officers with any pending vigilance/disciplinary case or criminal case - a clean clearance is mandatory. Officers who have been imposed major/minor penalties in the last 10 years - this may disqualify candidature. Officers who are not willing to serve at the specific NCLT Bench they are applying for. Officers who may want to withdraw their application after submission - withdrawal is NOT permitted. Officers whose cadre controlling authority will not issue Cadre Clearance or will not relieve them upon selection. Candidates expecting absorption into NCLT as a right - it is entirely at NCLT's discretion. Private Secretary / Stenographer applicants who do not meet the prescribed shorthand and transcription speed norms.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes / What to Check Before Applying NCLT Deputation Recruitment 2026?
content_sections.text: Not sending the application through proper channel - applications sent directly by the officer without forwarding authority's signature will not be considered. Not attaching attested photocopies of ACRs/APARs for the last 5 years - mandatory; applications without this will be rejected. Not including the Cadre Clearance Certificate from the cadre controlling authority - compulsory. Not attaching Vigilance Clearance Certificate and Integrity Certificate - mandatory documents. Not disclosing major/minor penalties of last 10 years - non-disclosure is suppression of material fact. Applying for a Bench where you are unwilling to serve - the selected candidate must serve only at that specific Bench. Not verifying that your current pay level and years of regular service meet the exact eligibility condition for the applied post. Private Secretary / Stenographer Grade-I / PA applicants not verifying their shorthand speed certification before applying. Cashier applicants not confirming completion of cash and accounts training. Submitting application after the last date (60 days from Employment News publication) - late applications will not be considered.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply for NCLT Deputation Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates shall apply offline only using Prescribed Application Proforma (Annexure-II). Applications must be submitted through proper channel only - via the Employer/Head of Office/Forwarding Authority. Applications sent directly by the candidate without being forwarded through proper channel will not be considered. Incomplete applications or those not accompanied by required documents will not be considered. Steps to Apply: Step 1: Download Application Proforma (Annexure-II) and eligibility conditions (Annexure-I) from www.nclt.gov.in under "Career". Step 2: Fill in Annexure-II completely. Attach self-attested passport size coloured photograph. State clearly in Section 19 how you are best suited for the post. Step 3: Attach all required documents (see list below). Step 4: Submit the complete application to your Employer/Head of Office/Forwarding Authority for certification and forwarding. Step 5: Forwarding authority sends the complete application to the Secretary, NCLT on or before the last date. Documents Required (to be submitted through proper channel): Bio-data in Prescribed Proforma - Annexure-II (completely filled) Supporting certificates/documents for claimed educational qualifications including essential Bachelor's Degree Cadre Clearance Certificate - certifying the officer will be relieved immediately upon selection Attested photocopies of APARs/ACRs for the last 5 years - attested by Under Secretary rank or above Vigilance Clearance Certificate issued by the respective department Integrity Certificate issued by the respective department Details of major/minor penalties imposed during the last 10 years Employer/Head of Office forwarding certificate (as per Annexure-II format) confirming: no vigilance/disciplinary/criminal case pending; integrity certified; APAR enclosed; no penalty in last 10 years; cadre authority has no objection Send Complete Application By Post To: The Secretary, NCLT National Company Law Tribunal 6th Floor, Block No. 3, C.G.O. Complex Lodhi Road, New Delhi – 110003 For queries: Tel: 011-24361685 | Email: [email protected]

content_sections.heading: Important Instructions for NCLT Deputation Recruitment 2026 Candidates
content_sections.text: Applications must be submitted through proper channel only - via Employer/Head of Office/Forwarding Authority. Direct applications will not be accepted. Candidates who apply will NOT be allowed to withdraw their application subsequently. Candidates unwilling to join upon selection must NOT apply. Absorption into NCLT is NOT a matter of right. It will be done only based on NCLT's need and as per NCLT Recruitment Rules 2020. Absorption can be considered only after 1 year of satisfactory deputation. Vacancy position is tentative and may increase or decrease. Selected candidates will serve only at the specific NCLT Bench for which they are selected. GPRA benefit: Only officers already availing GPRA in their parent body can continue this benefit after joining NCLT on deputation. Others are not eligible to apply for GPRA on joining NCLT. Cadre authorities must verify that all particulars sent by their officers are correct as per official records before forwarding applications. Pre-01.01.2016 regular service will be treated as service in the corresponding level of the 7th CPC pay matrix for eligibility determination. NCLT Recruitment Rules 2020 are available on www.nclt.gov.in under "Career" - candidates must read these before applying. Applications not accompanied by all required documents, or received after the last date, will not be considered under any circumstances. All corrigenda/updates will be published on www.nclt.gov.in - visit regularly.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for NCLT Deputation Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to apply is 60 days from the date of publication in Employment News / Rozgar Samachar.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in NCLT Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 106 vacancies are available across multiple posts and NCLT benches in India.

faq.question: Q3. What is the mode of application for NCLT Deputation Vacancy 2026?
faq.answer: The application process is offline only and must be submitted through proper channel via the employer/forwarding authority.

faq.question: Q4. Who can apply for NCLT Deputation Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Only serving Central/State Government officers, UT employees, or officials from courts/tribunals/statutory bodies are eligible.

faq.question: Q5. What is the maximum age limit for NCLT Deputation posts?
faq.answer: The maximum age limit is 56 years as on the closing date of application submission.

faq.question: Q6. Is there any application fee for NCLT Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There is no application fee required for this deputation-based recruitment.

faq.question: Q7. What is the selection process for NCLT Deputation Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is based on scrutiny of applications, APAR/ACR records, experience, and assessment by a Selection Committee.

faq.question: Q8. What is the salary for NCLT posts in 2026 recruitment?
faq.answer: Salary ranges from Level-2 (₹19,900) to Level-13 (₹2,15,900) as per the 7th CPC Pay Matrix.

faq.question: Q9. What is the duration of deputation in NCLT Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The deputation period is initially 3 years or until further orders, whichever is earlier.

faq.question: Q10. Can candidates get permanent absorption in NCLT after deputation?
faq.answer: Absorption may be considered after 1 year of deputation, but it is not guaranteed and depends on NCLT requirements.
